The '''Nejma''' were an aquatic [[Species/Legends|species]] originally native to [[Eriscot]]. The Nejma relocated to the [[Water/Legends|water]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] to escape a cataclysm that struck Eriscot<ref name="gamer1">{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}}</ref> around five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Date">{{CSWECite|II|119|Iskalonian}} states that the [[Species/Legends|species]] constituting the [[Iskalonian]] cultural group were brought to [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] approximately five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Since "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series]]" establishes that the relocation of the Iskalonian species was prefaced by the cataclysms on the species' original [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]], the cataclysms must also have occurred around that [[Time/Legends|time]].</ref> and became a part of the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]], a cultural group of several aquatic species that had traveled to Iskalon with the aid of [[Unidentified spacefaring benefactors|spacefaring benefactors]].<ref name="gamer1">{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}}</ref> A very tall, [[Color/Legends|brown]]-skinned race that typically had wild orange hair, red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a long dorsal fin on their backs,<ref name="tegas" /> the Nejma were known as the "Honored Ones" by the rest of the School, and largely kept themselves isolated from wider Iskalonian society. Nejma created renowned artwork out of coral reefs, which often attracted treasure seekers and raiders.<ref name="gamer1" />
